title: Writer’s Block Is Part of Maggie O’Farrell’s Process
description:
When I brought Maggie O’Farrell’s The Hand That First Held Mine to the women at the Ahmanson Senior Citizen Center’s writing class, many of them working on their own short stories about motherhood and family, they couldn’t stop talking about Lexie—the ambitious, young, single mother who refuses the provincial life laid out for her and […]

The post Writer’s Block Is Part of Magg
hook: How Maggie O'Farrell writes women whose ambitions devour them whole—and why a senior writing class couldn't look away.

title: Rash Reading, Slow Watching with Virginia Woolf and Chantal Akerman
description:
In the hands of this writer and this filmmaker, “the mother” is someone with whom to have a lifelong conversation, a contested perspective through which to read the world.

The post Rash Reading, Slow Watching with Virginia Woolf and Chantal Akerman appeared first on Public Books.
hook: Virginia Woolf and Chantal Akerman teach us to read mothers as contested perspectives, not simple narratives.

title: You Who Forsake the Lord
description: William T. Vollmann charts the rise, ruin, and refashioning of America.
hook: William T. Vollmann maps America's ideological collapse with the obsessive precision of someone who refuses easy answers.

title: The Vanishing Library: Timothy Ely’s Odd Little Book from Outer Space
description: “The few words it contains are indecipherable, of no language. (The letters look Hebrew—if you don’t know Hebrew and have never seen Hebrew letters before.)”
hook: A mysterious artist crafted an indecipherable book from outer space. The Paris Review investigates what it means.

title: Kacey Musgraves' new album pairs well with a biography of '70s singer Gary Stewart
description: Musgraves' album Middle of Nowhere has the dramatic detail of good fiction. The same is true of Gary Stewart: I Am From the Honky-Tonks, Jimmy McDonough's portrait of a gifted but tragic performer.
hook: Kacey Musgraves' new album has the textured specificity of literary fiction—just like the tragic biography of a forgotten honky-tonk singer.
